name: Multi-Band Aperture Photometry and Color-Magnitude Diagram of Messier 12
description:
Perform a multi-band aperture photometry pipeline on the Messier 12 (NGC 6218) globular cluster to construct a calibrated color-magnitude diagram (CMD). Using the V-band (Vcomb.fits) and B-band (Bcomb.fits) images in ~/AstroImages/m12_cmd/, measure at least 20 bright, well-separated stars using an aperture radius of 8, an inner sky annulus of 15, and an outer sky annulus of 25. Save the measurements to ~/AstroImages/m12_cmd/v_band_measurements.csv and ~/AstroImages/m12_cmd/b_band_measurements.csv.

Create an analysis script at ~/AstroImages/m12_cmd/analyze_cmd.py to cross-match the bands (3-pixel tolerance) and the reference catalog (m12_catalog.csv, 5-pixel tolerance). The script must calculate photometric zero-points, apply them to the data, and export a calibrated photometry table to ~/AstroImages/m12_cmd/results/calibrated_photometry.csv with columns for star_id, x_pixel, y_pixel, V_inst, B_inst, V_cal, B_cal, and BV_color. Generate a CMD plot with B-V color on the x-axis and calibrated V magnitude on an inverted y-axis, saved to ~/AstroImages/m12_cmd/results/cmd_plot.png.

Write a summary report to ~/AstroImages/m12_cmd/results/photometry_report.txt containing the number of stars measured in each band, the calculated zero-points (ZP_V = <value> and ZP_B = <value>), the observed B-V color range, and the magnitude of the brightest measured star in calibrated V. Login credentials: username 'ga', password 'password123'.
